e2e.notes: Server (next start on :3010) died mid-run; all 6 failures are ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED infra flakes, not test defects. Iter 1 passed all 20 on the same suite. Playwright JSON file on disk (artifacts/e2e-1.json) was NOT overwritten by iter 2 - it still holds iter 1 data; counts here parsed from embedded JSON in stdout log at /tmp/pw2-output.log.

recommendations.description: Largest Contentful Paint marks the time at which the largest text or image is painted. [Learn more about the Largest Contentful Paint metric](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/lighthouse/performance/lighthouse-largest-contentful-paint/)
recommendations.title: Largest Contentful Paint

recommendations.description: Sum of all time periods between FCP and Time to Interactive, when task length exceeded 50ms, expressed in milliseconds. [Learn more about the Total Blocking Time metric](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/lighthouse/performance/lighthouse-total-blocking-time/).
recommendations.title: Total Blocking Time

recommendations.description: Speed Index shows how quickly the contents of a page are visibly populated. [Learn more about the Speed Index metric](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/lighthouse/performance/speed-index/).
recommendations.title: Speed Index

recommendations.description: A forced reflow occurs when JavaScript queries geometric properties (such as offsetWidth) after styles have been invalidated by a change to the DOM state. This can result in poor performance. Learn more about [forced reflows](https://developers.google.com/web/fundamentals/performance/rendering/avoid-large-complex-layouts-and-layout-thrashing#avoid-forced-synchronous-layouts) and possible mitigations.
recommendations.title: Forced reflow

recommendations.description: Polyfills and transforms enable older browsers to use new JavaScript features. However, many aren't necessary for modern browsers. Consider modifying your JavaScript build process to not transpile [Baseline](https://web.dev/articles/baseline-and-polyfills) features, unless you know you must support older browsers. [Learn why most sites can deploy ES6+ code without transpiling](https://philipwalton.com/articles/the-state-of-es5-on-the-web/)
recommendations.title: Legacy JavaScript

recommendations.description: [Avoid chaining critical requests](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/lighthouse/performance/critical-request-chains) by reducing the length of chains, reducing the download size of resources, or deferring the download of unnecessary resources to improve page load.
recommendations.title: Network dependency tree

recommendations.description: Requests are blocking the page's initial render, which may delay LCP. [Deferring or inlining](https://web.dev/learn/performance/understanding-the-critical-path#render-blocking_resources) can move these network requests out of the critical path.
recommendations.title: Render blocking requests

recommendations.description: Time to Interactive is the amount of time it takes for the page to become fully interactive. [Learn more about the Time to Interactive metric](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/lighthouse/performance/interactive/).
recommendations.title: Time to Interactive

recommendations.description: The maximum potential First Input Delay that your users could experience is the duration of the longest task. [Learn more about the Maximum Potential First Input Delay metric](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/lighthouse/performance/lighthouse-max-potential-fid/).
recommendations.title: Max Potential First Input Delay

recommendations.description: Resources are blocking the first paint of your page. Consider delivering critical JS/CSS inline and deferring all non-critical JS/styles. [Learn how to eliminate render-blocking resources](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/lighthouse/performance/render-blocking-resources/).
recommendations.title: Eliminate render-blocking resources

recommendations.description: Reduce unused JavaScript and defer loading scripts until they are required to decrease bytes consumed by network activity. [Learn how to reduce unused JavaScript](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/lighthouse/performance/unused-javascript/).
recommendations.title: Reduce unused JavaScript

recommendations.description: Polyfills and transforms enable legacy browsers to use new JavaScript features. However, many aren't necessary for modern browsers. Consider modifying your JavaScript build process to not transpile [Baseline](https://web.dev/baseline) features, unless you know you must support legacy browsers. [Learn why most sites can deploy ES6+ code without transpiling](https://philipwalton.com/articles/the-state-of-es5-on-the-web/)
recommendations.title: Avoid serving legacy JavaScript to modern browsers

recommendations.description: A large DOM will increase memory usage, cause longer [style calculations](https://developers.google.com/web/fundamentals/performance/rendering/reduce-the-scope-and-complexity-of-style-calculations), and produce costly [layout reflows](https://developers.google.com/speed/articles/reflow). [Learn how to avoid an excessive DOM size](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/lighthouse/performance/dom-size/).
recommendations.title: Avoid an excessive DOM size

recommendations.description: Consider reducing the time spent parsing, compiling, and executing JS. You may find delivering smaller JS payloads helps with this. [Learn how to reduce Javascript execution time](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/lighthouse/performance/bootup-time/).
recommendations.title: Reduce JavaScript execution time

recommendations.description: Consider reducing the time spent parsing, compiling and executing JS. You may find delivering smaller JS payloads helps with this. [Learn how to minimize main-thread work](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/lighthouse/performance/mainthread-work-breakdown/)
recommendations.title: Minimize main-thread work

gsapPlugins.registration: All plugins registered once client-side in src/gsap/registerPlugins.ts, each wrapped in try/catch. Club plugins attempted from 'gsap/*' then 'gsap-trial/*'; failures log a dev warning and the site uses documented fallbacks (Lenis for ScrollSmoother, manual span split for SplitText, strokeDasharray for DrawSVG, opacity crossfade for MorphSVG, TextPlugin for ScrambleText, CustomEase curves for bounce/wiggle).

mobileFallback.behavior: R3F Canvas replaced by static SVG core+5 orbit nodes animated via CSS keyframes; pinned sections revert to normal flow via ScrollTrigger.matchMedia; postprocessing disabled; GSAP durations scaled to ~0 under reduced-motion.

persona.name: Alex Chen
persona.tagline: Orchestrating web, mobile and cloud from a single command surface.

sections.name: Hero
sections.sceneState: Core scales 0.4->1.0 and pulses up to 1.0 with blue emissive; all 5 modules collapsed at origin (activation 0); no links; camera static at [0,0.6,7.5] fov 42 with slight dolly-in.
sections.title: ALEX CHEN — DEVELOPER CONTROL CORE

sections.name: About
sections.sceneState: Core eases to scale 0.9 pulse 0.6, tint shifts toward purple; modules expand from origin onto orbit with activation 0.3; rig rotation ramps 0->0.04 rad/s; camera drifts to [2.6,1.2,5.8] fov 40; links still 0.
sections.title: Five years shipping production software.

sections.name: Skills
sections.sceneState: Core state=active, sinusoidal pulse amp 0.8, tint cycles across module colors; modules fire sequentially (FE, BE, DevOps, Cloud, Mobile) activation 0.3->1.0 with CustomBounce overshoot; connection lines draw in one-by-one via uDraw 0->1; camera arcs to [0,2.4,4.4] top-down via MotionPath.
sections.title: Five modules. One runtime.

sections.name: Projects
sections.sceneState: Core state=execute, scale pulses 0.9<->1.1 on card snap, chromatic aberration spikes; rig tilt 0.18->0.32; active module for current project at 1.0 others 0.4; only active link 1.0 others 0.3; camera slides to [-3.2,0.4,5.0].
sections.title: Execution log — 5 builds.

sections.name: Experience
sections.sceneState: Core state=timeline, scale 1.1->0.75 collapsing, pulse 0.4; rig radius 3.2->4.6, rotation briefly 0.12 rad/s then settles 0.06; all links alpha 0.7; camera pulls back to [0,-0.3,8.2] fov 50 looking up.
sections.title: Roles, in order.

sections.name: Contact
sections.sceneState: Core state=signal, scale 0.75->1.25 expands, pulse 1.0 then CustomWiggle strobe; modules all activation 1.0 and collapse inward (radius 4.6->1.6) on final 0.05; links full 1.0 with wiggle flicker; bloom 0.9->1.6; camera zooms to [0,0,3.2] fov 36.
sections.title: Ping the core.

stack.animation: GSAP 3 (ScrollTrigger, ScrollSmoother, SplitText, DrawSVG, MorphSVG, MotionPath, Flip, Observer, Inertia, ScrambleText, Physics2D, PhysicsProps, CustomEase, CustomBounce, CustomWiggle, GSDevTools, EaselPlugin, PixiPlugin, TextPlugin) — Club plugins guarded with try/catch fallbacks
stack.deploy: Vercel (edge API route, static page, preview per PR)
stack.fonts: Geist Sans + JetBrains Mono via next/font
stack.styling: Tailwind CSS + CSS Modules + clsx + tailwind-merge
stack.testing: Playwright E2E + visual snapshots, Lighthouse CI, Vitest unit

testScenarios: hero headline visible and contains ALEX CHEN
hero terminal lines finish typing within 2s
about stats counters reach final values after 16vh scroll
skills 5 ModuleBadge elements activate in sequence (frontend, backend, devops, cloud, mobile)
skills all 5 badges active at section end
projects horizontal pin scroll cycles data-project-active through all 5 projects
projects clicking a card opens detail panel
experience 4 timeline nodes present and DrawSVG path stroke-dashoffset reaches 0
scroll-journey: data-section-active transitions hero->about->skills->projects->experience->contact with no console errors
a11y: axe-core reports zero critical violations and tab order is complete
reduced-motion: Canvas replaced by SvgCoreFallback and content remains readable
lighthouse mobile: LCP<3000, CLS<0.1, TBT<300, perf>=0.75
visual snapshots match baseline at 0/50/100% per section on desktop and mobile

issues: Skills screenshot does not show ModuleBadge + SkillBars — captured frame is at the intro/title state of a pinned section, so the skill overlays never came into view. Either (a) the pinned-scroll sub-slide needs to be triggered after scrollIntoView via an additional window.scrollBy or timeline advance, or (b) the overlay needs to be visible on first sub-slide.

Projects screenshot shows the section title + carousel dot-paginator but no actual project card with neon border is in-frame. Same pinned-section capture issue as Skills: cards likely live in later sub-slides.

Connection lines are not readable as a glowing web in the Projects screenshot. The Experience screenshot shows the clearest line (faint dotted arc). Thickness/double-render may need another bump, or the lines need to draw between visible module positions rather than off-viewport anchors.

Contact 'Ping the core.' headline is not visible in the captured frame — the bright expanding ping-ring glow is overlapping/obscuring the headline zone, or the headline z-index/position puts it under the radial gradient.
Hero headline wraps as 'SYSTEM // BOO' / 'T' at 1440w — consider a tighter tracking or a smaller font-size breakpoint so 'SYSTEM // BOOT' fits on one line (or intentionally breaks at '//').

Screenshot spec uses a fixed 1400ms wait after scrollIntoView; pinned / scroll-triggered sub-slides (skills, projects) need extra scroll events to actually reveal the badge/bar/card content — consider scripting a window.scrollBy sequence per pinned section.

about.notes: GlassPanel rendering well with purple border glow, 'Five years shipping production software.' headline, eyebrow 'SYS.INIT // 00 -- OPERATOR PROFILE', metrics row (120+ SHIPPED / 99.70% UPTIME / 5 STACKS MASTERED), paragraph copy, and three colored 3D blobs (green/cyan/orange) visible behind/beside the panel. Glass fidelity good, typography clean. (a)85 (b)85 (c)75 (d)80 (e)70.

contact.notes: Form landed: GlassPanel with Name / Email / Message fields and teal 'SEND SIGNAL' button, eyebrow '// 06 SIGNAL TRANSMISSION' is present at top, subtext 'Open to full-stack and 3D-web roles...' present, closing pill 'LET'S BUILD SOMETHING POWERFUL.' present. A bright blue radial glow (expanding ping ring visible mid-animation) fills the panel center. MISSING/OBSCURED: the large 'Ping the core.' headline is not visible — either it is covered by the ping-ring glow or rendered behind/outside the captured frame. Orange and cyan module shapes flank the panel. (a)85 (b)85 (c)75 (d)78 (e)80.

experience.notes: 'A short history of shipping.' headline with eyebrow 'SYS.TIMELINE // 05 -- HISTORY'. 3D shapes are arranged along an arc with a faint dotted connection line curving through — this is the clearest instance of the double-rendered connection line, though still subtle. No timeline cards visible in this frame (likely below the viewport). (a)82 (b)65 (c)78 (d)78 (e)70.

hero.notes: 12-col grid landed: 'SYSTEM // BOOT' headline is left-weighted with boot-log terminal panel on the right showing green 'running review v2.1 / loading module 0/5 / ready -- press enter to init' lines. Eyebrow '//BOOT.00', subhead 'The Developer Control Core', CTA 'SCROLL TO INIT ->' all present. Glow/dropshadow on headline is visible and a faint horizontal scrim sits behind it. Palette on-brand (deep navy, cyan+magenta accents). Minor concern: headline wraps awkwardly ('BOOT' / 'T' on second line) at 1440w and terminal panel looks a bit small/cropped in upper-right corner. (a)90 (b)78 (c)70 (d)85 (e)88.

projects.notes: Eyebrow 'SYS.EXECUTION // 04 -- SELECTED WORK', 'Execution.' magenta headline, subhead about '5 production systems', and a vertical dot-paginator on the right edge (suggesting a carousel) all rendered. HOWEVER no neon-bordered project cards are actually visible in the frame — only the large central sphere with play icon and the floating module shapes. Horizontal pinned carousel implied by the dot-nav but the card track itself is empty/offscreen in this screenshot. Connection lines: not clearly readable as a 'glowing web' between core and modules; at best, faint aura around the central sphere. (a)80 (b)55 (c)80 (d)75 (e)60.

skills.notes: CRITICAL GAP: screenshot shows only the section header state ('Five modules. One runtime.' + 3D module cluster) — NO ModuleBadge or SkillBars visible. Expected gradient-filled bars with % labels are absent in the captured frame. Eyebrow 'SYS.ACTIVATION // 03 -- MODULES ONLINE' is present. The 3D scene (sphere, torus knot, cubes) is vibrant but the whole point of iter2 (badge + bars overlay) did not render in the captured viewport — either the sub-slide hasn't activated by scroll-start or the overlay lives further down the pinned section. (a)75 (b)40 (c)85 (d)70 (e)55.
